transcript.text: That's all for now. Don't forget Sport on 4 tomorrow morning with Tony Lewis. For now, though, a very good night to you all.
Thank you, Simon. Now here's the time signal for 10 o'clock.
On 275 and 285, also in stereo on VHF, this is National Radio 1.
Well, hello there. Thomas the Vance here, TV on the radio, and once again, it's the Friday Rock Show.
Tonight from the archives, the Fab Four, the Four Mock Tops, John Paul, George and Ringo,
the Beatles as they were on the air at the BBC in 1964.
Also, the first ever session by a new heavy band who call themselves Samson.
We've got classic cuts by the Moody Blues, Cream, Velvet Underground and Janis Joplin,
and new releases by The Damned, Martha and the Muffins and Gloria Mundy.
Also, of course, our Friday Night Connection.
Lots of mail this week, but of course, we have a winner.
Stay tuned for that.
But tonight, we start with Ian Hunter.
